title: Add Two Numbers
description: Given two numbers a and b add them up and return the output

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def add_two_numbers(a, b):
    # Write your code here
    # Return the sum of a and b
    return a + b

import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(add_two_numbers(a, b))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(a + b)

title: Find Maximum of Two Numbers
description: Given two integers a and b, determine and return the greater of the two numbers.
hints: Check which number is greater using if condition

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def find_max(a, b):
    # Write your code here
    if a > b:
        return a
    return b

import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(find_max(a, b))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(max(a, b))

title: Check Even or Odd
description: Given an integer n, determine whether it is even or odd.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def check_even_odd(n):
    if n % 2 == 0:
        return "Even"
    return "Odd"

import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read())
print(check_even_odd(n))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read())
print("Even" if n % 2 == 0 else "Odd")

title: Sum of First N Natural Numbers
description: Given a number n, return the sum of first n natural numbers.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def sum_n(n):
    return n * (n + 1) // 2

import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read())
print(sum_n(n))

title: Reverse a Number
description: Given an integer n, return its reverse.
hints: Use division to remove last digit

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def reverse_number(n):
    sign = -1 if n < 0 else 1
    n = abs(n)
    rev = 0
    while n > 0:
        rev = rev * 10 + (n % 10)
        n //= 10
    return rev * sign

import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read())
print(reverse_number(n))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
n = int(sys.stdin.read())
sign = -1 if n < 0 else 1
n = abs(n)
rev = 0
while n > 0:
    rev = rev * 10 + (n % 10)
    n //= 10
print(rev * sign)

title: Find Minimum of Two Numbers
description: Given two integers a and b, return the smaller of the two numbers.
hints: Check which number is smaller using an if condition.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def find_min(a, b):
    # Write your code here
    if a < b:
        return a
    return b

import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(find_min(a, b))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(min(a, b))

title: Calculate Square of a Number
description: Given an integer n, return its square.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def square(n):
    # Write your code here

import sys

print(square(n))

hints: Multiply the number by itself three times.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:

import sys

title: Check Positive Negative or Zero
description: Given an integer n, determine whether it is Positive, Negative, or Zero.

title: Swap Two Numbers
description: Given two integers a and b, output them after swapping their values.
hints: Store one value before overwriting it.

examples.explanation: After swapping, a becomes 10 and b becomes 5.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
def swap_numbers(a, b):
    # Write your code here
    temp = a
    a = b
    b = temp
    return f"{a} {b}"

import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
print(swap_numbers(a, b))

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys
input_line = sys.stdin.read()
a, b = map(int, input_line.split())
a, b = b, a
print(f"{a} {b}")
